title: anti-patterns.yaml — registered legacy-shape detection patterns
description: Machine-readable spec of the .dw-lifecycle/scope-discovery/anti-patterns.yaml wire format consumed by the scope-discovery `dw-lifecycle check-anti-patterns` gate. Each entry registers ONE legacy code shape that a canonical primitive replaces; the scanner walks the source tree and reports every file matching the shape outside the canonical implementation file. The runtime parser (plugins/dw-lifecycle/src/scope-discovery/anti-patterns-registry.ts → parseEntry) is the authoritative validator; this schema mirrors its contract for editor autocomplete + inline validation and is referenced by the Phase 9 doctor rules. v1 implements ONLY the `regex` pattern type; the optional `type:` field accepts `regex` or absence (treated as `regex`). Extension to `glob` / `ast-grep` / `ts-morph` pattern types is tracked at audiocontrol-org/deskwork#285.

anti_patterns.description: The registered legacy shapes. Order is not significant; ids must be unique. Empty list is valid (the scanner exits 0 with a `registry empty` acknowledgement).

AntiPatternEntry.description: One registered legacy shape. `id` (kebab-case) is the primary key. `added_in` anchors the registration to a specific commit so reviewers can trace when the pattern was added. `primitive` + `from` name the canonical replacement (used in the report's `replacement:` line). `shape_regex` may be a single regex string OR a list of regex strings (multi-pattern fingerprint — see `min_distance`). `excludes_paths` skips files matching the listed globs BEFORE applying the shape patterns. `canonical_file` names the canonical primitive's source-of-truth file; the scanner auto-excludes that file from the entry's match AND verifies the file exists at scan start (fails loud if missing — typically caused by a git rename without registry update). `message` is the multi-line operator-facing replacement instruction shown beneath each finding.

id.description: Kebab-case primary key (lowercase letters, digits, hyphens; no leading/trailing/double hyphens). Unique across the registry. Operators reference this id when authoring `excludes_paths:` overrides or when filing follow-up issues.

type.description: Pattern-type discriminator. v1 accepts `regex` or absence (treated as `regex`). Other types (`glob` / `ast-grep` / `ts-morph`) are reserved for the v2 dispatcher tracked at audiocontrol-org/deskwork#285 — the runtime parser rejects any other value loudly so adopters who pre-author v2 entries get an actionable error.

added_in.description: Git short-or-full hash (7-40 lowercase hex characters) of the commit that registered this anti-pattern. Anchors the registration to a specific point in history so the operator can trace context.

primitive.description: Name of the canonical replacement (hook / component / function name). Surfaces in the report's `replacement:` line.

from.description: Import path of the canonical replacement (e.g. `'@/hooks/useExportDialogLifecycle'`). Surfaces in the report's `replacement:` line.

shape_regex.description: Regex(es) that fingerprint the legacy shape. Either a single regex string (single-pattern entry; any match flags the file) OR a non-empty list of regex strings (multi-pattern fingerprint; every pattern must occur within `min_distance` lines of a seed match in pattern 0). Patterns are compiled with `gm` flags (global + multi-line).

min_distance.description: Max line-gap between matches for a multi-pattern fingerprint to count as a hit. Defaults to 50 when omitted. Ignored for single-pattern entries. Must be a positive integer.

excludes_paths.description: Optional list of literal-path-or-glob strings. Each candidate file's CWD-relative POSIX path is tested against every pattern; a match skips the file for THIS entry only. `excludes_paths` matching nothing is intentionally NOT an error — it keeps the registry tolerant to file renames the operator will fix on the next run. Empty list is equivalent to omitting the field. Glob syntax matches `util/glob.ts` (supports `**`, `*`, `?`, brace alternation).

canonical_file.description: Optional canonical implementation file path; auto-excluded from match regardless of excludes_paths. CWD-relative POSIX path of the primitive's source-of-truth file. When set, the scanner: (a) verifies the file exists at scan start (failing loud with the entry id + missing path if not — typically caused by a git rename without registry update); (b) auto-excludes that file from this entry's match (its body IS the legacy shape, by construction). Path matching is byte-exact — no glob, no directory expansion. Independent of `excludes_paths`: both apply when set together (union semantics). Non-empty string or absent only — empty string is rejected as a malformed declaration.

message.description: Multi-line operator-facing replacement instruction. Surfaces beneath each finding (indented by 2 spaces). Free-form prose; conventionally explains WHY this shape was retired and HOW to replace it.

status.description: Loop status. One of pending / blessed / cursed / ignore / tracked-holdout / withdrawn. Default `blessed` for hand-authored entries (the pre-Loop behavior). Scanners enforce `blessed` + `cursed` entries; every other status is skipped (pending awaiting triage, ignore acknowledged false-positive, tracked-holdout deferred to a tracked issue, withdrawn overturned by an audit finding). See plugins/dw-lifecycle/src/scope-discovery/util/catalog-status.ts for the full lifecycle spec.

provenance.description: provenance block tracking where the entry came from. Synthesized to install-seed when absent (back-compat with pre-Loop registries). REQUIRED when `status:` is set to anything other than `blessed`. The `withdrawn` status carries an additional invariant: `provenance.context` MUST start with `audit-finding-` (the reversibility primitive's contract).

audit_history.description: REVERSE provenance link to the audit-log. List of audit-log Finding-IDs that referenced this entry over time. Optional (back-compat: pre-Task-10 entries omit it). The `provenance-orphaned-entries` doctor rule cross-checks each id against in-progress feature audit-logs to surface broken references.

CatalogStatus.description: Shared Loop-status discriminator (catalog status + provenance). Applied uniformly to every catalog entry type so scanners can filter on it without learning per-registry semantics. See util/catalog-status.ts for the canonical spec.

Provenance.description: Shared Loop-provenance shape (catalog status + provenance). Tracks where the entry came from (operator-authored / orchestrator-agent / llm-judge-proposed / install-seed / promoted-from-candidate), when it was authored, by whom, in what context, and an optional evidence link. The `context` field carries a tracking ref (typically `scan-run-id-<id>` or `audit-finding-<id>`); for `withdrawn` status, the value MUST start with `audit-finding-`.

context.description: Tracking ref (e.g., `scan-run-id-<id>`, `audit-finding-<id>`). REQUIRED when status === 'withdrawn'; value must start with `audit-finding-` in that case.

evidence_link.description: URL or repo-relative path to justifying evidence.

RegexString.description: Non-empty regex source string. Validated at parse time by attempting to compile it with the `gm` flags. The parser raises a descriptive error naming the entry, the offending field, and the underlying regex error if compilation fails.
